scope.summary: Implement a Schematic render mode in ClothViewport that renders the garment as a technical flat drawing with seam edges, topstitch lines, grain direction arrows, and optional measurement callouts, captured as a TailorVisualCapture with render_mode = 'schematic'
acceptance_criteria: RenderMode::Schematic renders the garment mesh as a flat filled silhouette with a solid outline color and no PBR shading
Seam edges (from SeamConstraintRecord pairs) are drawn as dashed lines in a distinct color from the panel outline
Grain direction arrows are drawn per panel at the panel centroid, oriented by PanelSpec::grain_angle_deg
Topstitch/fold internal lines (from GarmentSpec internal guide lines per MT-132) are drawn as dot-dashed lines
tailor_capture_frame accepts render_mode: 'schematic' and returns a TailorVisualCapture with the schematic PNG
The schematic render is implemented as an additional wgpu pipeline in ClothViewport, not a CPU-side SVG generator, so it reuses the existing offscreen texture readback path
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: capture a 3-panel garment in schematic mode; assert output PNG contains no specular/PBR shading and shows distinct seam dashes on panel boundaries
scope.risk_if_missed: Without Schematic render, Tailor cannot produce tech-pack drawings for fashion production workflows. Designers sourcing production to manufacturers need technical flat illustrations with measurement callouts. This is a baseline fashion-industry deliverable that MD delivers natively.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
